title: Parser-Surface Reconciliation And Baseline Shim
objective: Implement the smallest bounded follow-on after packet 020e.1 by reconciling the real PyP6Xer Reader surface with Apex loader expectations and adding a narrow baseline raw-section shim for PROJBASELINE or BASELINEPROJECT rows when the parser does not expose them directly, without adding bridge routes, PM UI behavior, schedule-write surfaces, or broad parser rewrites.
constrained_outputs: One bounded Apex-owned compatibility layer that normalizes real Reader names such as activities, wbss, and relations into the loader's governed import surface
One narrow raw-section shim for PROJBASELINE or BASELINEPROJECT rows when the parser does not expose baseline collections directly
Focused validation evidence proving the loader can truthfully emit baseline entries from a contract-compliant input without introducing synthetic baseline fabrication
validation_steps: Confirm real Reader surface drift is reconciled explicitly rather than assumed away
Confirm baseline raw-section handling remains narrow, import-only, and governed by the existing non-overload rules
Confirm no bridge route, PM UI, SQL, or schedule-write changes are introduced
Confirm a contract-compliant baseline-bearing fixture or synthetic test raw text can drive deterministic loader assertions
sql_permission_note: Packet 020g-a is parser-surface and loader compatibility work only. No SQL, no bridge changes, no PM UI changes, and no write-surface expansion are authorized.
